```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(GGally)
```

```{r}
# duomenu atsisiuntimo nuoroda: https://archive.ics.uci.edu/dataset/468/online+shoppers+purchasing+intention+dataset

df <- read.csv("data\\online_shoppers_intention.csv", header=TRUE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
```


```{r}
missing_table <- data.frame(
  Praleista = colSums(is.na(df))
)

missing_table <- data.frame(
  Kintamasis = rownames(missing_table),
  missing_table,
  row.names = NULL
)

knitr::kable(missing_table, caption="Praleistų reikšmių skaičiai\\label{tab:missing}")
```


```{r}
# manau reikės aprašyti, kad pas mus 0 reikšmių (FALSE) yra daug daugiau nei 1 reikšmių (TRUE)
# duomenų disbalansas


print(df["Revenue"] %>% group_by_all %>% count)

print(1908/(10422+1908)*100)
```

```{r}
# reikės truputi perdėlioti lentelę, nes dabar labai plati gavosi
# reikės nuimti SpecialDay	OperatingSystems	Browser	Region	TrafficType, nes jie labiau kategoriniai manau

summary_tbl <- df %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      list(
        Minimumas = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        `1-asis kvartilis` = ~quantile(.x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        Mediana = ~median(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        Vidurkis = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        `3-iasis kvartilis` = ~quantile(.x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        Maksimumas = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
      ),
      .names = "{.col}__{.fn}"
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = c("Kintamasis", "Statistika"),
    names_pattern = "^(.*)__(.*)$"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Kintamasis, values_from = value)

knitr::kable(
  summary_tbl,
  caption = "Kiekybinių kintamųjų skaitinės charakteristikos\\label{tab:char}",
  digits = 2,
  format.args = list(decimal.mark = ",", big.mark = " ")
)
```



```{r}
# BounceRates ir ExitRates pakylimas ant 0.20 keistai atrodo

numeric_cols <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)]

for (col in numeric_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = !!rlang::sym(col))) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#2C7FB8", color = "white") +
    labs(
      title = paste(col),
      x = "Reikšmė",
      y = "Dažnis"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  
  print(p)
}
```

## Kategorinių kintamųjų stulpelinės diagramos

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
# Mėnesius reikės išrikiuoti pagal kalendorių ir lankytojo tipą pagal dažnį

categorical_cols <- names(df)[!sapply(df, is.numeric)]
categorical_cols <- setdiff(categorical_cols, "Revenue")

for (col in categorical_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = !!rlang::sym(col), fill = !!rlang::sym(col))) +
    geom_bar() +
    labs(
      title = paste(col),
      x = col,
      y = "Dažnis"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none")
  
  print(p)
}
```

## Tankio diagramos pagal Revenue

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
df_with_revenue <- df %>% mutate(Revenue = factor(Revenue))

for (col in numeric_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(df_with_revenue, aes(x = !!rlang::sym(col), fill = Revenue, color = Revenue)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Skirstinys pagal Revenue:", col),
      x = col,
      y = "Tankis"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  
  print(p)
}
```



## Smuikinės diagramos pagal Revenue

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
# per pratybas buvo paminėti violin plots

df_long <- df %>%
  mutate(Revenue = factor(Revenue)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(numeric_cols),
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

for (col in numeric_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(df %>% mutate(Revenue = factor(Revenue)), 
              aes(x = Revenue, y = !!rlang::sym(col), fill = Revenue)) +
    geom_violin(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Skirstinys pagal Revenue:", col),
      x = "Revenue",
      y = col
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
  
  print(p)
}
```


## Kategorinių kintamųjų dažnių lentelės

```{r}
# other yra labai mažai, tai gal vertėtų išimti

visitor_freq <- df %>%
  group_by(VisitorType) %>%
  summarise(Dažnis = n()) %>%
  mutate(Procentai = round(Dažnis / sum(Dažnis) * 100, 2))

knitr::kable(
  visitor_freq,
  caption = "VisitorType dažnių lentelė\\label{tab:visitor}",
  align = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

```{r}
weekend_freq <- df %>%
  group_by(Weekend) %>%
  summarise(Dažnis = n()) %>%
  mutate(Procentai = round(Dažnis / sum(Dažnis) * 100, 2))

knitr::kable(
  weekend_freq,
  caption = "Weekend dažnių lentelė\\label{tab:weekend}",
  align = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

```{r}
revenue_freq <- df %>%
  group_by(Revenue) %>%
  summarise(Dažnis = n()) %>%
  mutate(Procentai = round(Dažnis / sum(Dažnis) * 100, 2))

knitr::kable(
  revenue_freq,
  caption = "Revenue dažnių lentelė\\label{tab:revenue}",
  align = c("l", "r", "r")
)
```

```{r}
visitor_revenue <- df %>%
  group_by(VisitorType, Revenue) %>%
  summarise(Dažnis = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Revenue, values_from = Dažnis, values_fill = 0)

knitr::kable(
  visitor_revenue,
  caption = "VisitorType ir Revenue kryžminė lentelė\\label{tab:visitor_revenue}"
)
```

```{r}
weekend_revenue <- df %>%
  group_by(Weekend, Revenue) %>%
  summarise(Dažnis = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Revenue, values_from = Dažnis, values_fill = 0)

knitr::kable(
  weekend_revenue,
  caption = "Weekend ir Revenue kryžminė lentelė\\label{tab:weekend_revenue}"
)
```
